content:
TL;DR: ChatGPT, Claude, and Perplexity name only 3-5 brands per answer. AI visibility measures how often yours is one of them -- across mention rate, sentiment, share of voice, and citation rate. GEO techniques can increase your citation rate by up to 40% (Princeton/IIT Delhi, ACM KDD 2024).

The shift nobody built a dashboard for

SEO gave us rankings. Ten blue links. Position 1 through 10.

AI search gives you a conversational answer that names three brands -- maybe five if the query is broad. If your brand is not one of them, you do not have a ranking problem. You do not exist in that buyer's discovery journey.

Google AI Overviews hit 2.5 billion monthly active users. Google AI Mode surpassed 1 billion monthly users in just 12 months, with queries more than doubling every quarter (Google I/O 2026, May 19, 2026). These are not emerging surfaces. They are the search surface.

AI visibility is the metric that measures your presence on them.

What is AI Visibility?

AI Visibility is the frequency, accuracy, and sentiment with which AI assistants -- ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, Google Gemini, Google AI Overviews, Google AI Mode -- mention, recommend, or cite a brand in response to user queries.

It is measured across five core metrics:

Metric
What It Measures
Benchmark

Mention Rate
% of relevant queries where your brand appears
40%+ for your category

Position
Where you appear in the answer (1st, 2nd, 3rd)
Top 2 consistently

Sentiment
Positive / neutral / negative characterization
80%+ positive

Share of Voice
Your mentions vs competitor mentions
25%+ in your niche

Citation Rate
How often AI links to your content as a source
15%+ of mentions

How this is different from SEO

SEO ranks pages. Ten positions, you can land anywhere in the top 10 and still get traffic.

AI visibility picks winners. Three to five brands per answer, winner-take-most.

The ranking factors are completely different:

•
SEO: keywords, backlinks, page speed, domain authority

•
AI visibility: brand search volume (0.334 correlation, #1 predictor), multi-platform presence, E-E-A-T signals, structured data in static HTML

That last point matters a lot if you are building on React or any SPA. Schema must be in prerendered HTML. If your Organization or FAQPage schema only exists in a React Helmet tag injected at runtime, AI crawlers (ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Googlebot) will not see it.

<!-- This works for AI crawlers -->
<script type="application/ld+json">
{
"@context": "https://schema.org",
"@type": "FAQPage",
"mainEntity": [{
"@type": "Question",
"name": "What is AI visibility?",
"acceptedAnswer": {
"@type": "Answer",
"text": "AI visibility is the frequency, accuracy, and sentiment with which AI assistants mention your brand in response to user queries."
}
}]
}
</script>

Enter fullscreen mode

Exit fullscreen mode

// This does NOT work for AI crawlers
// Schema injected only via React Helmet at runtime
// will not be seen by ClaudeBot or PerplexityBot
import { Helmet } from 'react-helmet';
<Helmet>
<script type="application/ld+json">{JSON.stringify(faqSchema)}</script>
</Helmet>

Enter fullscreen mode

Exit fullscreen mode

We run a React SPA at Astiva AI and confirmed schema visibility via:

curl -A "ClaudeBot" https://astiva.ai/blog/what-is-ai-visibility

Enter fullscreen mode

Exit fullscreen mode

Schema must appear in the raw HTML response. If it is not there, it does not count.

7 signals AI models use to select brands

From The Digital Bloom's 2025 AI Visibility Report, testing ChatGPT citation behavior across thousands of queries:

•
Brand search volume -- 0.334 correlation coefficient, the #1 predictor. Brands people Google are the brands AI recommends.

•
Content authority and E-E-A-T -- named authors, cited sources, accurate data.

•
Multi-platform presence -- brands on 4+ platforms are 2.8x more likely to be cited by ChatGPT.

•
Structured data -- FAQPage + Organization schema = 2.5x more likely to be cited (Zyppy SEO study).

•
Content freshness -- 65% of AI bot traffic targets content published within the past 12 months.

•
Sentiment and review quality -- G2, Capterra, Reddit threads all feed AI brand perception.

•
Source diversity -- consistent brand descriptions across Wikipedia, publications, and review sites.

One contrarian finding: backlinks alone do not drive AI visibility. A brand with 10,000 backlinks from low-traffic sources and zero search volume is less visible to AI than a brand with 500 high-authority backlinks that drive real search demand.

The 3 highest-impact GEO optimization methods

From the Princeton-IIT Delhi GEO study (Aggarwal et al., ACM KDD 2024, arXiv:2311.09735), tested across 10,000 queries:

1. Cite credible external sources -- +40% visibility

Citing .edu domains, peer-reviewed research, and government data produced a +41% lift on Position-Adjusted Word Count. For lower-ranked pages it was +115%.

AI models use your citations as a trust proxy. Content that cites primary research is treated as more authoritative than content making the same claims without attribution.

2. Add specific, attributed statistics -- +28% visibility

Not "studies show 70% of users..." -- that gets ignored or flagged as unverifiable.

Yes: "Semrush 2025 found 34.5% of ChatGPT queries activate web search."

The attribution matters as much as the number itself.

3. Answer-first content structure -- +15-30% visibility

72.4% of cited posts include a 40-60 word answer capsule placed directly after the H1 or primary H2. 44% of all citations come from the first third of the content (ALM Corp, 2026).

Bury the answer in sentence three and you fail AI extraction entirely.

What does not work: keyword stuffing scored -9% in the Princeton study. It actively hurts AI citation rates.

Real result: 0% to 40% mention rate in 45 days

We ran a GEO optimization for a mid-market B2B SaaS company in marketing automation (Astiva beta program, Q1 2026). Starting point: 34 published pages, 0 pages scoring above 15/25 on our GEO content framework.

Five fixes applied to their 8 highest-traffic pages:

• Answer-first opening paragraphs (60 words or fewer)

• FAQPage + Article + Person schema in static HTML JSON-LD

• 3-5 sourced statistics per page with inline links

• 2-3 H2 headings converted to question format

• One comparison table with specific numbers per page

Results after 45 days across 40 tracked prompts:

Platform
Before
After
Change

Perplexity
0/40 (0%)
16/40 (40%)
+40%

ChatGPT
0/40 (0%)
11/40 (27.5%)
+27.5%

Claude
0/40 (0%)
9/40 (22.5%)
+22.5%

Perplexity responded fastest because it uses real-time web search. ChatGPT and Claude are slower -- they rely on training data that refreshes every 2-6 months.

Lesson for devs: Perplexity is the closest thing to a staging environment for AI visibility. Test GEO changes there first for the fastest feedback loop.

Per-platform discovery behavior (June 2026)

Platform
Discovery Method
Time to Influence

ChatGPT
Training data + web browsing (Plus/Pro)
2-6 months

Claude
Training data + web search (3.5+)
2-6 months

Perplexity
Real-time web search
Days to weeks

Google AI Overviews
Google Search index (2.5B users)
Weeks

Google AI Mode
Google Search + AI layer (1B users)
Weeks

Gemini
Google Search + training data
Weeks

Single-platform optimization is not a strategy. A brand can hold 70% mention rate on Perplexity and 20% on ChatGPT for the exact same queries because the discovery mechanisms are entirely different.

How to build your AI visibility baseline in 5 steps

• Pick 20-50 seed queries (navigational, categorical, comparison)

• Run each on ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, Gemini -- record: appeared? position? sentiment?

• Calculate mention rate, average position, sentiment ratio, share of voice vs top 3 competitors

• Set up automated monitoring -- 50 prompts x 4 platforms = 200 individual runs per week manually

• Track changes after each optimization action -- AI visibility fluctuates 40-60% with model updates, so average over 4+ weeks before drawing conclusions

What we built to solve the monitoring problem

This is the problem Astiva AI was built to solve. We are building the measurement layer for how AI platforms recommend brands -- tracking citations, share of voice, sentiment volatility, and competitive displacement across 10 platforms: ChatGPT, Claude, Google Gemini, Google AI Overviews, Google AI Mode, Perplexity, Grok, Meta AI, DeepSeek, and Mistral AI. One dashboard. 7 AISO metrics. Native GA4 revenue attribution from the Growth plan.

Run a free AI brand visibility audit on your brand

Full guide with schema walkthrough and the 25-Point GEO Content Framework: astiva.ai/blog/what-is-ai-visibility
